seoTitle: See Payment History in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Look up a customer's full payment history in QuickBooks Online, filter by date or type, and export a clean transaction statement.
intro: Whether you're confirming when an invoice was paid or reviewing a client's full payment habits, QuickBooks Online's customer transaction hub makes it easy to pull up detailed records. This guide shows how to filter and audit payment history for any customer.

steps.heading: Open the customer directory
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Customers, to see your full client list.

steps.heading: Open the client's transaction hub
steps.body: Click on a specific customer to open their consolidated transaction history panel.

steps.heading: Filter for payments
steps.body: Use the Type dropdown above the grid and uncheck everything except Payments to isolate settlement records.

steps.heading: Set a date range
steps.body: Adjust the Date filter to a custom range or a full multi-year view depending on what you need to review.

steps.heading: Review or export
steps.body: Click a payment row to see which invoices it settled, or click Run Report to generate a printable history you can export as PDF or Excel.

faq.question: Can I see which invoice a specific payment was applied to?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking a payment row expands the form to show exactly which invoices were linked and closed by that payment.

faq.question: How do I filter payment history by date?
faq.answer: Use the Date dropdown above the customer's transaction grid to select a custom range or multi-year view.

faq.question: Can I export a customer's payment history?
faq.answer: Yes, click Run Report from the customer's header and use the export button to save it as PDF or Excel.

faq.question: Can I look up payment history without opening the customer profile?
faq.answer: You typically need to open the specific customer's transaction hub to filter their payment records directly.
